IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: We are removing 7 sections of overhead electricity cables and an overhead transformer and replacing them with new underground cables and a ground-mounted transformer. We will also be replacing and upgrading 5 poles. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate that some customers may have experienced an unplanned power cut in the last 6 months. To allow our contractors to safely dismantle a section of the electricity network, we will need to temporarily disconnect the generator on 8th July 2026. Once the work on 8th July 2026 is finished, the temporary generator will be reconnected. After all planned works have been completed, we will then disconnect the generator on 9th July 2026 and return your supply to the main power network. This work is taking place near Hadlow Down Road. A road closure will be in place on the 7th-9th of July 2026 to allow the works to be carried out safely. Access to your property will always be provided.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 18:46 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely cut trees away from the overhead cables. By carefully planning this work in advance, we have been able to minimise the duration of the power interruption. This essential work will cover approximately 100 metres of tree cutting across 1 sections of the overhead network. Doing this helps reduce the risk of future unplanned power cuts and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place near Walsham Road.

Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, a longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be carried out. This work is taking place in Aylesbury Road.

Steps.Message:
Hi again. I know it can be uncomfortable having a power cut in higher temperatures, but as the evening draws in, there are ways to cool down.

Your property will still hold the heat built up over the day once the evening arrives, so we'd recommend opening windows and doors once the sun has gone in; outside temperatures should fall quickly and you can let some fresh air in.

Getting to sleep can also be a bit harder without power to run fans or air conditioning, but sleeping under a sheet instead of a duvet might help. As always, I'll keep you updated with news.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 19:50 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Adam at 18:45 - We're very sorry we've had to switch off your electricity. Our estimated time to have your power back on is between 19:30 and 20:30, however, this may need to change later depending on how our works progress. If you'd like more information about why we sometimes have to switch power off to work; here's a short video https://UKPN.short.gy/Isol. We'll keep you updated with progress.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Theo at 08:13 - Our engineers have restored power to all 56 properties in the ME14, ME20 & TN25 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have replaced safety equipment that had operated on our overhead network.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 07:22 - Thanks for your patience this morning.

Our staff are currently checking our network equipment throughout the area. In addition to this, they are liaising closely with our control room to find safe and reliable ways to divert power around any problems and get everyone restored.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the ME14/ME20 & TN25 area to investigate the power cut affecting 1091 properties. Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive, even more so if you are vulnerable. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress but for any concerns or questions, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. For more information on how we support vulnerable customers just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we need to connect a new substation to the electricity network at the request of a third party. This equipment controls and delivers electricity into your area. As a regulated electricity network operator, we are required to complete this work and we will do so as safely and efficiently as possible while working to minimise disruption to our customers. To connect the new equipment and complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off. Once completed this will adhere to the third party request, ensure the network can uphold the increased electricity usage in your area and reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate some customers may have experienced unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place near Dunmow Road. Two way traffic lights will be in place to allow the works to be carried out safely. Access to your property will always be provided.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

To complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off. To ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our contractors Avidety may carry out preparation works prior to the power cut. This work is taking place near Hadlow Down Road. A road closure will be in place on the 7th-9th of July 2026 to allow the works to be carried out safely. Access to your property will always be provided.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Theo at 11:11 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the overhead cables. This work will cover 7 sections of the network. Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, a longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be carried out. Completing this work in one visit helps reduce the risk of future unplanned outages and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place on Trap Lane.

Steps.Message:
Hi, this is Simon from the messaging team at UK Power Networks. I'm sorry for the power cut today; I know the lights going off can be disruptive.

We know that some customers affected by todays power cut may have had other issues recently, and I appreciate this is frustrating. High voltage cabling can cover a wide area, so sometimes properties very close to you may have had power cuts that you didn't experience.

I know you may not have had any previous power cuts, but if you have, we often get progress updates from our field staff management about investigations into repeat issues, and our Customer Services team will try to contact everyone affected by them to explain what's happening.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Simon at 11:32 - Our engineers are currently investigating a report of possible damage on our overhead cables. Further to this, our field staff have just told us that 425 properties now have their lights safely back on after making another diversion. I know there's still some properties affected, so I'll keep sending updates but as mentioned, if you're back up and running now, you can always stop receiving SMS messages from us on our website here: https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP. Otherwise, I'll have some more news for you soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We became aware of this power cut at 10:20. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut, but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly. Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.
Steps.Name: We are restoring customers automatically

Steps.Message: We have engineers on their way. As this is affecting your area, they will not need to attend your property. Rest assured once they arrive, they’ll work as quickly as is safely possible to get your power back on.
Steps.Name: Engineers on their way

Steps.Message: Engineers are now on site, carrying out investigations into the fault. Once they have completed their assessment, they will carry out repairs.
Steps.Name: Investigating & fixing

Steps.Message: All power in the area should be back on. We’re really sorry for any disruption this caused.
Steps.Name: Power back on

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: We were investigating a high voltage overhead electricity line fault which caused a power cut in the local area.

Please call us on 08003163105 (free from landlines and mobiles).

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 18:01 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.

Steps.Message:
Our staff have now arrived on site in the North Street RM1 area. They will start running tests at our substation to see how the local network is operating.

It's quite common for power to be restored briefly and go off again; this is our engineers using testing equipment to energise different sections of our network - this helps to narrow down any possible issues.

Our cabling network is often broken down by smaller sites, and our staff may also check these to test for any problems, and they may also walk the cabling route to see if there are any signs of malfunction that have appeared on the surface. Our time frame hasn't been adjusted, so for now I'll let you know more news as soon as our staff update us.

Sent by Simon at 07:33.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Simon at 07:14 - Our staff are on the way to the North Street RM1 area, and our estimated time frame to have power back on is currently between 11:00 and 12:00. However, this could change once our staff have assessed the local network.

We believe the power cuts you have been experiencing are caused by a fault with one of the cables. Developing faults can start very small, and so can be hard to detect but each power cut usually makes it easier to see, which means our specialist staff can detect it, and work on the repair to prevent further outages.

I'll let you know once our teams arrive.

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

Sent by Will at 19:15 - Our engineers have now restored power to all affected customers in the Harlow, CM19 area, by diverting power around the underground network fault. We know a power cut can be difficult; we're sorry for any disruption this unplanned power cut caused.  

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations.

To talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.

Sorry your power has gone off again today; our engineers previously diverted the electricity around the faulted part of the network remotely. However, this has not held and we believe there may be an underlying issue.

We have your information from the earlier power cut so will continue to send progress updates to you until all power is back on. Sent by Adam at 18:31.

Sent by Adam at 18:21 - Our engineers have restored power to all 2303 properties in the Harlow, CM19 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have diverted power around the underground network fault.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.

Our engineers have arrived on site in the Harlow, CM19 area to investigate the power cut affecting 491 properties. 

Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off. 

We know that a power cut can be very disruptive, even more so if you are vulnerable. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress but for any concerns or questions, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. For more information on how we support vulnerable customers just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of our equipment in your area. This has identified 5 cables and 10 poles that need upgrading. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate you may have been impacted by an unplanned power cut in the last 6 months. To complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off to upgrade the equipment. To ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our contractors Avidety may carry out preparation works prior to the power cut. Avidety will need access to the inside of your property to conduct safety checks in the morning before we switch off the power and we will need to do another check when we turn the power back on. This work is taking place on Church Lane.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our contractors need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely cut trees away from the overhead cables. By carefully planning this work in advance, we have been able to minimise the duration of the power interruption. This essential work will cover approximately 375 metres of tree cutting across 5 sections of the overhead network. Doing this helps reduce the risk of future unplanned power cuts and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place in multiple locations near Grovely Lane.

Hi, it's Decasta from the messaging team at UK Power Networks. I understand how difficult it can be without power and the additional worries this can bring.

For both our business customers and those at home, we will work as quickly and safely as possible to restore supplies. If you need any additional support or have any concerns, please call us on 105 or tweet us @ukpowernetworks.

Steps.Message:
Thanks for your patience today. We know a power cut can be difficult.

Many properties have gas hobs, which can still be used in a power cut for cooking, or heating water for drinks and sterilisation of baby bottles. The spark to ignite the hob will not work however, so if you do use it please take extra care when using other ignition sources like matches or lighters. Even in daytime, the light in your house may be low in a power cut, so only do this if you are confident it can be done safely.

If you have any questions or concerns, our team are always on hand to help.

Logs.Comment:
Sent by Adam at 16:38 - Our engineers have restored power to all 2086 properties in the Bury St. Edmunds & Stowmarket area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have removed a large tree branch from our overhead lines.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.

Our engineers have arrived on site in the Bury St. Edmunds & Stowmarket area to investigate the power cut affecting 224 properties. 

Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off. 

We know that a power cut can be very disruptive, even more so if you are vulnerable. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress but for any concerns or questions, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. For more information on how we support vulnerable customers just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR

Sent by Adam at 15:42 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an overhead network fault that initially interrupted power to 1361 properties.

However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 224 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment.

We're currently estimating power will be on between 18:00-19:00, but this may change depending on what our staff find.

Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage affects multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you expect. We're arranging staff to attend ASAP, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting electricity. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to get as many customers back on as we can.

Once available, our engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, they will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will work to locate the issue and make repairs, which may take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing but we'll always give you the clearest information we have.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of our equipment in your area. This has identified 7 poles that need replacing and upgrading. We will also be replacing and transferring overhead cables, service cables and a transformer over to the new poles. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future. To complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off to upgrade the equipment. To ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our contractors may carry out preparation works prior to the power cut. This work is taking place in land off Church Road.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area as we appreciate some customers may have experienced unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the overhead cables. This work will cover 5 sections of the network. Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, a longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be carried out. Completing this work in one visit helps reduce the risk of future unplanned outages and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable. This work is taking place near Hockford Close.

Steps.Message:
Our staff have now arrived on site in the Broadway area. Due to there being multiple power cuts over a short space of time, our engineers are going to be testing if the network is still healthy.

They will start at the substation, and often apply specialist equipment that may turn your power back on for a brief period, so don't worry if your power comes on and goes off again. They will also check smaller sites which run along our cabling route, these break our network down into smaller sections and can help pinpoint any possible problems.

Our estimated time frame of 21:00 and 22:00 hasn't changed, so I'll let you know more once our staff update us. Sent by Will at 19:12.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: These works were originally planned for the 25th of June 2026 but had to be cancelled due to the extreme heat. We are diverting overhead electricity cables, to underground electricity cables to allow for a new development to be established. This work is following a customers request. Doing this will ensure our network is safe whilst works commence in the area and will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely carry out the work. As a regulated electricity network operator, we are required to complete this work and we will do so as safely and efficiently as possible while working to minimise disruption to our customers. This work is taking place off Gresley Way.
